_meta.purpose: Schools Pilot — Students/Youth edition (WPY) behaviour library. Read by api/migrate/import_school_behaviours.php.
_meta.structure: 72 PERMA-H behaviours (12 per scope/direction set × 6 sets) + 25 generic UP + 25 generic DOWN
_meta.voice: Secondary-school appropriate (years 7-12). Adolescent identity, peer dynamics, study load, screen / social media, future planning. Spin Up: present imperative ('Notice...', 'Help...', 'Bring...'). Spin Down: past tense observation ('Spoke harshly...', 'Doom-scrolled...'). Refine titles to match local school vocabulary if needed.
_meta.drafted_by: Claude — 2026-06-26 Phase F draft, mirrors the WPK library structure with youth-appropriate content
_meta.rerun_safety: Importer skips activities already present via (consumer, sub, scope, direction, dimension, title) dedupe. Re-running after a library edit only inserts new/changed-title rows; existing rows keep their qr_token.

behaviours.title: Notice three things you're grateful for today
Savour a small win without rushing past it
Lose track of time on something that lights you up
Lean into a strength when something feels hard
Speak to yourself the way you would to a friend
Forgive yourself for being too hard on yourself
Do the right thing when no one's watching
Spend ten minutes on something that genuinely matters to you
Finish what you said you'd finish
Take a small risk on something new
Move your body for at least 20 minutes today
Put the phone down an hour before sleep
Beat yourself up over a setback
Sat with a bad mood without checking in on it
Doom-scrolled instead of starting the work
Bailed on a commitment you made to yourself
Compared yourself harshly to someone you follow online
Hid a mistake instead of owning it
Quit something meaningful because it got hard
Performed an opinion you don't actually hold
Took the easy way out on a real choice
Talked yourself out of trying
Skipped meals because of stress
Stayed up scrolling when you needed sleep
Share a small joy with your group
Notice when a classmate is struggling and acknowledge it
Help a classmate make sense of something tricky
Contribute to a group project without waiting to be asked
Include someone who's been on the edge of the group
Repair a small rift with a friend before it grows
Push back when something unfair happens to someone
Voice a perspective that needs to be heard
Cheer on a classmate's win out loud
Stick with a group through a hard stretch
Invite a friend to move with you
Check in on a friend who's been quiet lately

Volunteer for something that makes the school better
Bring an idea that improves how things actually work
Build a bridge across year levels
Mentor a younger student through something they're stuck on
Represent the school well off-site

Help with a project that outlasts your time at the school
Lead an initiative that actually matters
Keep shared spaces inviting and clean
Look out for a younger student's safety

Brought all materials needed for class

Tried again after getting feedback

Followed through on a commitment

Held a door open for someone

Asked for help when stuck instead of pretending
